confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Score of 4 reflects high symbolic-order strength at the war's end: the Union's constitutional order survived, and Lincoln gave it a powerful new sacral legitimation through democratic-rebirth framing. Not maximum because the order's strength was achieved through enormous material sacrifice and was not yet institutionally consolidated; Reconstruction collapse suggests the symbolic renewal did not fully take hold.

reviewNote: Score value 4/5 defensible: anti-sacrificial-restraint mechanism (Lincoln's reframing of sacrifice as national rededication rather than enemy annihilation) supports elevated but not maximum symbolic-order-strength. Confidence 0.63 is proportionate to uncertainty factors (chain requires expansion; Reconstruction failure complicates the score by showing the anti-sacrificial architecture was not durable). Ready for human-reviewed pending chain expansion to ≥4 passages.
